intitule: Passive Voice in Context: Identifying Usage

enonce.texte:
Read the following paragraph about a situation in Cameroon and answer the questions below.

"Last week, a new community library was built in our village. The funds were raised by a local NGO. Many trees were planted around the library. Unfortunately, some books were damaged during the transport. However, a solution has been found by the village chief and the youth leader."
images: A modern community library built in a rural Cameroonian village, with newly planted trees around it.

questions.texte: Analyze the use of the passive voice in the sentence "A new community library was built in our village." Why is the passive voice preferred here instead of the active voice?
questions.reponse: The passive voice is preferred because the agent (the people who built the library) is either unknown or less important than the action itself (the building of the library) and the object (the library). The focus is on what happened to the library, not who built it.

questions.texte: Compare the sentences "The funds were raised by a local NGO" and "Many trees were planted." In which sentence is the agent specified, and why?
questions.reponse: In the first sentence, the agent ('by a local NGO') is specified because it is important information to give credit or show the source of the action. In the second sentence, the agent is not mentioned because it is obvious (people from the village) or unimportant; the focus is entirely on the action of planting the trees.

questions.texte: Justify the use of the passive voice in the sentence "a solution has been found by the village chief and the youth leader." Would the meaning change if we used the active voice?
questions.reponse: The passive is used here to put the emphasis on the 'solution' as the main topic of discussion. While the agent is important (chief and youth leader), the sentence is primarily stating that a solution exists. Using the active voice ('The village chief and the youth leader have found a solution') would shift the focus from the solution to the people who found it. The meaning is the same, but the emphasis is different.

intitule: Applying Passive Voice in Real-Life Situations

enonce.texte: Imagine you are a journalist for a school newspaper. You are writing short news headlines or reports. Rewrite the following active sentences into passive voice to make them more suitable for a news report, where the action or result is more important than the doer.

questions.texte: Rewrite this active sentence in the passive voice: "Someone stole three smartphones from the school library yesterday."
questions.reponse: Three smartphones were stolen from the school library yesterday.

questions.texte: Rewrite this active sentence in the passive voice: "The government will launch a new scholarship program for girls next year."
questions.reponse: A new scholarship program for girls will be launched by the government next year. OR A new scholarship program for girls will be launched next year. (The agent can be omitted if obvious).

questions.texte: Rewrite this active sentence in the passive voice: "The school principal has postponed the final exams due to the strike."
questions.reponse: The final exams have been postponed due to the strike.

intitule: Problem-Solving: Choosing Between Active and Passive

enonce.texte: You are writing a formal report for your civics class on the topic "Road Safety in Our Town." For each situation below, decide whether to use the active or passive voice and justify your choice. Then, write the sentence.
images: A busy, chaotic intersection in a Cameroonian city with no traffic lights.

questions.texte: You want to state a fact that a new speed bump exists on the road near the school. The person who installed it is the municipal council, but this is general knowledge. Which voice is better, and what is your sentence?
questions.reponse: The passive voice is better because the focus is on the speed bump, not on who installed it. The sentence is: 'A new speed bump has been installed on the road near the school.'

questions.texte: You want to announce that a specific person, 'Mr. Atangana, the transport delegate,' made a new rule. The rule is that all bus drivers must wear seatbelts. Which voice is better to give him clear credit for this action, and what is your sentence?
questions.reponse: The active voice is better because the agent (Mr. Atangana) is a specific, important person who should be credited with the action. The sentence is: 'Mr. Atangana, the transport delegate, has announced that all bus drivers must wear seatbelts.'

questions.texte: During a heavy rainstorm, a tree fell and blocked the main road. Nobody knows who removed it, but the road is now clear. You are reporting that the road is open again. Which voice is more appropriate, and what is your sentence?
questions.reponse: The passive voice is more appropriate because the agent (the person who removed the tree) is unknown and irrelevant. The important information is the result: the road is clear. The sentence is: 'The fallen tree has been removed, and the road is now open.'

intitule: Evaluating Passive Voice in Official Documents

enonce.texte:
Read this excerpt from a fake official school policy. Then evaluate the use of the passive voice by answering the questions.

"It has been decided that school uniforms will be mandatory for all students. Punctuality must be respected at all times. Any student caught cheating during exams will be disqualified. The reasons for these rules have been explained by the disciplinary council."

questions.texte: Evaluate the effect of using "It has been decided that..." instead of "The disciplinary council has decided that..." How does this choice impact the tone of the policy?
questions.reponse: Using 'It has been decided that...' creates a more formal, impersonal, and authoritative tone. It makes the decision seem like an objective fact or a rule from an anonymous authority, rather than a choice made by specific people. This can make the rule seem less debatable and more absolute.

questions.texte: The policy uses a passive modal: "Punctuality must be respected." Propose an active voice alternative. Which version is more common in rules and regulations, and why?
questions.reponse: An active voice alternative is: 'All students must respect punctuality.' The passive version ('Punctuality must be respected') is much more common in official rules and regulations. This is because the passive voice focuses on the action (respecting punctuality) and the thing being regulated (punctuality itself), rather than the people who must follow the rule. It makes the rule sound more universal and less like it is directed at a specific group.
